designPillars.description: Every danger should be readable before it hurts the player.
Good movement, target priority, and resource timing should make the same stage feel easier.
A tense wave should be followed by a wave where the player can spend skills and feel powerful.
Each stage introduces one major combat question, then remixes it.

components.name: Open Loop
components.use: Movement learning, horde payoff, wide 360-degree combat.

components.name: Ring Bowl
components.use: Combo pressure and central positioning.

components.name: Gate Lane
components.use: Dash reads, frontal pressure, bruiser gates.

components.name: Broken Lanes
components.use: Ranged priority and approach route decisions.

components.name: Trap Field
components.use: Moving safety zones and chase bait.

components.name: Duel Board
components.use: Boss pattern reading and punish windows.

components.name: Final Remix Arena
components.use: Layered endgame checks without new rules.

components.name: Fodder Feed
components.use: Low-threat enemies for hit feel, combo, and resource recovery.

components.name: Anchor Elite
components.use: A durable enemy that gives the crowd a center of gravity.

components.name: Flanker
components.use: Fast enemies that punish tunnel vision.

components.name: Ranged Tax
components.use: Long-range enemies that become costly if ignored.

components.name: Trap Clock
components.use: Delayed area danger that forces movement decisions.

components.name: Commander Focus
components.use: High-priority enemy that makes the wave harder while alive.

components.name: Boss Primer
components.use: Small wave before a boss to refill resources and teach the pattern space.

components.name: Final Remix
components.use: Multiple learned rules combined into one end-stage exam.

components.name: Combo B
components.use: Clear while reaching at least B rank combo pressure.

components.name: Fast Clear
components.use: Clear under a target time for extra reward weight.

components.name: Priority Kill
components.use: Kill a specified threat before the rest of the wave collapses.

components.name: Clean Dodge
components.use: Avoid a boss signature attack and counter within the punish window.

components.name: Resource Finish
components.use: Finish the final elite or boss with a skill or ultimate.

stageBlueprints.coreQuestion: Can the player move, aim, and keep basic attacks flowing?
Can the player hold the center and keep combo pressure under elite presence?
Can the player save resources for a boss after dense elite waves?
Can the player read fast dash attacks and stop greedy attacks early?
Can the player identify and delete ranged threats before the field becomes expensive?
Can the player chase skirmishers without being baited into delayed danger?
Can the player manage a horde while finding and killing commanders first?
Can the player slow down after horde combat and punish only real boss openings?
Can the player rotate through every learned answer without a new rule being introduced?
